secondary_archetype: Internal Systems / GTM & Finance Platform TPM

level_signals: 2+ years of software engineering, systems engineering and/or technical program management experience
Scope involves large-scale technical programs but minimum bar is relatively low at 2 years

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years of software engineering, systems engineering and/or technical program management experience.
Excellent analytical skills; you digest complex information, can distill it, and make recommendations and proposals.
You have a track record of providing program management during all phases of software development from kick-off to delivery, demonstrating critical thinking.
You have very strong written and verbal communication skills and build strong relationships with stakeholders.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in technical program management, in GTM and Financial systems.
You have experience managing technical software products from infancy to scale.
You have experience designing and building a multi-product platform.
You're a creative thinker who loves collaborating across the company with engineering, analytics, marketing, design, and sales to experiment and build superior products.
You're scrappy, you do what it takes to get it done.

scope.surface: Internal Systems — platform and product solutions for internal users (Sellers and Finance Engineering)
scope.business_model: internal platform / enterprise tooling within a B2B financial infrastructure company
primary_metrics_inferred: KPIs and success criteria for large-scale technical programs
Frictionless execution for Sellers and Finance Engineering users
core_responsibilities: Working with teams across the organization to understand pain points and work to create solutions which span multiple domains.
Define and produce high quality written proposals, communications and documentation.
Execute on technical programs that require deep systems and engineering level engagement.
Partner with Engineering Managers, Tech Leads, Engineers, business partners, Product Managers, and other Technical Program Managers to define, scope and drive programs to conclusion.
You will identify and resolve program trade-offs in partnership with engineering.
Develop, implement, and iterate on program management techniques, frameworks, and KPIs to achieve goals with well defined success criteria.
Elevate the execution muscle of engineering teams around you.
Help influence peers / stakeholders and build consensus while dealing with ambiguity.
Create widely circulated plans, driving consistency, clarity, and building alignment across teams.

green_flags: Clear team mission and scope described (Internal Systems, Sellers and Finance Engineering)
Explicitly states preferred qualifications are a bonus and encourages candidates who meet minimum requirements to apply
Relatively accessible minimum bar (2+ years) for a TPM role at a major fintech
Cross-functional exposure spans engineering, analytics, marketing, design, and sales
red_flags: No compensation or equity information disclosed
Location and work authorization not specified anywhere in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
SQL listed as a preferred qualification without context on depth expected — may be more important than implied
Very brief preferred qualifications section offers limited signal about actual seniority expectations

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 2+ years minimum and IC-level responsibilities; no explicit level stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from Stripe's B2B financial infrastructure description and internal platform context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from KPI references in responsibilities; not explicitly listed)
location.type (not stated anywhere in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: Location is entirely unstated in the JD body — no city, no remote/hybrid/onsite designation provided. Marked as 'unclear'.
Minimum requirement of '2+ years' in software engineering, systems engineering, AND/OR TPM is a broad combined qualifier — unclear whether any single 2-year track suffices or combination is expected.
SQL scripting appears as a standalone bullet under preferred qualifications with no context on expected depth or use cases.
